assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observable outputs respond to legal input changes without assuming internal state.
Checks focus on input/output consistency rather than cycle-accurate timing.
Output c equals (a + b) modulo 2^N for all legal input combinations.
cases.goal: Validate functional correctness of the adder across the full input space without assuming timing.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Assumes purely combinational behavior as indicated by the contract.
No internal state or latency is inferred.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known non-control inputs: ['a', 'b']
Drive a wide variety of random legal values on inputs a and b, covering both typical and extreme patterns.
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.
Observe c after inputs have settled; no fixed-cycle latency is assumed.

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
Output c correctly reflects overflow behavior (wrap‑around) when adding maximum operand values.
cases.goal: Exercise boundary conditions that may trigger overflow or sign‑bit changes.
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
Focuses on min/max patterns and zero/one patterns for inputs a and b.
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['a', 'b']
Apply zero, one, maximum, and just‑below‑maximum values to a and b.
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.
Observe c after inputs have stabilized; no latency assumptions are made.

expected_properties: When a and b are both set to the maximum N‑bit value, c wraps around to (2^N‑2) modulo 2^N, demonstrating correct overflow handling.
cases.goal: Confirm that the adder truncates overflow bits and produces the expected modular result.
notes: Uses the same combinational observation model as the contract.
preconditions: Inputs a and b are driven with the maximum representable value (all bits = 1).
stimulus_intent: Drive both a and b with the maximum N‑bit value to provoke overflow.
timing_assumptions: Check c after inputs settle; no fixed latency is assumed.

expected_properties: Successive independent input vectors produce corresponding independent output values without residual effects.
cases.goal: Verify that rapid successive changes on a and b produce correct, independent outputs each time.
notes: Even though the design is combinational, this case checks for any unintended latch or state retention.
preconditions: A sequence of distinct input pairs is applied with sufficient settling time between them.
stimulus_intent: Apply a series of varied (a,b) pairs back‑to‑back and monitor c after each change.
timing_assumptions: Each output is observed after the corresponding inputs have stabilized.
